#ask why are hamburgers called hamburgers when there's no ham?
#gpt why are hamburgers called hamburgers when there's no ham?
isn't it named after the German city?

Hamburger meat is ground meat used to make hamburgers. It’s typically beef that has been minced/ground and sold as raw ground beef (often labeled with a fat percentage, e.g., 80/20). It may also be made from other meats (e.g., turkey, chicken, pork) if labeled accordingly.
Hamburgers are typically made from ground beef (usually beef). Some “hamburgers” may use other meats such as ground turkey, ground chicken, pork, or a plant-based meat substitute.
Yeah it is named after a city in germany

Hamburgers are named after the city of Hamburg, Germany, as they originate from the "Hamburg steak," a dish of minced beef brought to the US by German immigrants in the 19th century. The "-er" suffix denotes someone or something from that place, and the dish later evolved into a sandwich in America.

#gpt are there any more kmarts in the usa

Yes, there are still Kmart stores in the USA, but their number is much smaller than in the past.  Key facts: - Kmart stores continue to exist, primarily in limited locations and often as store-sized footprints rather than widespread regional presence. - Many original Kmart locations were closed over the last decades (notably during major restructuring periods in the 2000s and late 2010s), and several stores were converted to other retailers or closed permanently. -

There is no single universal “too much” number for peanuts, because risk depends on (1) your total daily calories, (2) total fat/salt intake, (3) your body size, and (4) whether you have peanut allergy.  Key practical limits used in nutrition guidance 1) Calories (most common “too much” issue) - Peanuts are calorie-dense: about 160–170 kcal per 1 ounce (28–30 g) of peanuts. - If you routinely exceed your daily calorie needs, “too much” effectively becomes whatever a

#gpt how much peanut butter is too much to eat ͏

#gpt how much peanut butter is too much to eat
